title: Distinguish agent vs human actors in activity entries across mutating tk commands
description:
Context: activity entries (internal/tick/store.go Activity struct, actor field) currently get their actor from whatever the save path passes; when empty it defaults to the tick owner (store.go:91-93). Only 'tk note' supports --from agent|human (cmd/tk/cmd/note.go). When a Claude orchestrator closes/updates/approves ticks, the activity feed shows the same actor as human actions - there is no way to tell who (or what) did what.

Deliverable: a consistent actor mechanism for all mutating commands (close, update, approve, reject, create, block/unblock, label):
1. Add a TK_ACTOR environment variable read once in the CLI layer; when set, it is passed as the actor on every save/activity write. This lets an orchestrator export TK_ACTOR=orchestrator (or agent:<name>) for the whole session without per-command flags.
2. Add an --actor flag to close and update that overrides TK_ACTOR for one invocation.
3. Precedence: --actor flag > TK_ACTOR env > existing default (tick owner). 'tk note --from' keeps working unchanged.

Test cases:
- TK_ACTOR=orchestrator tk close <id> --reason x -> activity entry actor="orchestrator"
- TK_ACTOR=orchestrator tk close <id> --actor human-pete -> actor="human-pete"
- no env, no flag -> actor falls back to current behavior (tick owner)
- TK_ACTOR set on tk update --status in_progress -> the 'start' activity entry carries the actor

Run: go test ./internal/tick/... ./cmd/tk/...

Files likely touched: cmd/tk/cmd/close.go, cmd/tk/cmd/update.go, cmd/tk/cmd/root.go (or wherever shared flag/env plumbing lives), internal/tick/store.go (only if the actor plumbing needs widening), plus command tests.

Note: do NOT add a new tk subcommand for this (no main.go switch changes needed - this is flags/env on existing commands).
acceptance_criteria: TK_ACTOR env and --actor flag set the activity actor with documented precedence; default behavior unchanged when neither is set; go test ./... green
